title_en: Diamond Necklace Keeps Perfect Record in Prix de Diane
description: Diamond Necklace remained unbeaten after winning her second classic of the season in the Prix de Diane (G1) June 14.

description: This week's Report from Japan takes a look at some of Flightline's 2-year-olds set to hit the track this year.

title_en: Meisho Tabaru Wins Takarazuka Kinen, Eyes Arc Bid.
description: Meisho Tabaru earned entry to both the Breeders' Cup Turf (G1T) and Australia's Cox Plate (G1) with a victory in the Takarazuka Kinen (G1) June 14 at Hanshin Racecourse, but might more likely be spending the autumn in France.

title_en: Mizumi Powers Home in Summertime Oaks
description: Mizumi scores in the $100,500 Summertime Oaks (G3) June 13 at Santa Anita Park, giving Hall of Fame trainer Bob Baffert his record sixth victory in the race.

title_en: Freedom's Not Free Catches Sumter in Daytona Stakes
description: Freedom's Not Free, a stakes-winning 3-year-old turf horse last year in California, wins the June 13 Daytona Stakes (G3T) at Santa Anita Park in his first start as a 4-year-old.

title_en: Luv Your Neighbor Breaks Through With Delaware Oaks Win
description: Luv Your Neighbor wins her first graded stakes race in the $295,000 Delaware Oaks (G3) June 13 at Delaware Park.

title_en: Diamond Necklace Formidable in Prix de Diane
description: Diamond Necklace will attempt to become the first filly trained outside of France to achieve a significant treble, while keeping up the relentless pace achieved by Aidan O'Brien with his 3-year-olds this classic campaign.

title_en: Dona Clota Prevails in Robert G. Dick Memorial
description: Dona Clota holds off a late charge from Alluring Angel in the Robert G. Dick Memorial Stakes (G3T) June 13 at Delaware Park.

title_en: Will Walden Talks About The Talent of La Rascasse
description: Trainer Will Walden discusses the promise of Resolute Racing's Monomoy Girl Overnight Stakes winner La Rascasse.

title_en: Burnham Square Tested But Holds On to Win Chorleywood
description: Grade 1 winner Burnham Square continues his raid of long-distance turf tests in Kentucky with a hard-fought neck triumph over Chapman's Peak in the fourth running of the $217,500 Chorleywood Stakes June 13 at Churchill Downs.

title_en: Wilkes: Burnham Square's Chorleywood mWin 'A Gut Check'
description: Trainer Ian Wilkes describes a close battle won by his horse, Burnham Square, in the June 13 Chorleywood Stakes at Churchill Downs.

title_en: East Avenue Nips Bishops Bay in Salvator Mile
description: A little more than a year after his nose loss in the 2025 Blue Grass Stakes (G1) at Keeneland, Godolphin's East Avenue comes out on the winning end of a tight finish in the $156,000 Salvator Mile Stakes (G3) June 13 at Monmouth Park.

title_en: Gimme a Nother Flashes to Victory in Eatontown
description: Newstead Stables' Gimme a Nother makes a winning return from a nine-month layoff to claim the Eatontown Stakes (G3T) June 13 at Monmouth Park.

title_en: Mitts Is First Winner for Gretzky the Great
description: Mitts, in his second career start, is placed first by disqualification giving his sire Gretzky the Great his initial winner in a 2-year-old maiden special weight race June 13 at Gulfstream Park.

title_en: Top Trainers, Horsemen Set for Start of Colonial Downs
description: Colonial Downs will host its annual summer meet beginning June 25 and continuing until Sept. 7. Featuring a blend of veteran and emerging talent, the diverse roster of horsemen is expected to create a competitive and dynamic racing season.

title_en: Croix du Nord Hunts Another Top-Notch Win in Takarazuka
description: Croix du Nord seeks a third straight grade 1 win and a free pass to either the Breeders' Cup Turf (G1T) or Australia's Cox Plate (G1) in the June 14 Takarazuka Kinen (G1T) at Hanshin Racecourse.

title_en: Lanerie to Retire at End of Churchill Spring Meet
description: Jockey Corey Lanerie announces he will retire at the conclusion of the Churchill Downs spring meet after a 35-year career.

title_en: Biancone Back to Royal Ascot With 'Little Rocket Ship'
description: Trainer Patrick Biancone sends out "little rocket ship" juvenile Celtic Dispute ahead of the Queen Mary Stakes (G2) at the Royal Ascot meet, hoping to secure his first victory at the prestigious racecourse.

title_en: Santa Anita Adds Racing June 15 After Power Failure
description: The conclusion of the winter/spring season at Santa Anita Park has been extended to June 15 following a shortened June 11 card, when races 6-9 were canceled because of a significant power failure.

title_en: Bishops Bay Looks to Defend Title in Salvator Mile
description: Last year's Salvator Mile Stakes (G3) winner Bishops Bay returns June 13 to defend his title in the $150,000 race at Monmouth Park.

title_en: Veteran Rothfire Carries Top Form Into Stradbroke
description: Trainer Rob Heathcote will have two chances to capture Queensland's premier handicap when Rothfire and Abounding line up in the June 13 Stradbroke Handicap (G1) at Eagle Farm Racecourse.

title_en: Ward Has High Hopes for Royal Ascot Contingent
description: Wesley Ward believes he is bringing some heavy artillery to the Royal Ascot meet next week, and has particularly lofty hopes for Outfielder in the Commonwealth Cup Stakes (G1).

title_en: Aqueduct Bids Farewell on Closing Weekend
description: Aqueduct Racetrack will honor its final closing weekend June 27-28 with an assortment of farewell celebrations that look back on decades of memorable Thoroughbred racing.

title_en: Tampa Bay Downs Announces Retirement of Flynn
description: Tampa Bay Downs announces June 11 the retirement of longtime vice president of marketing and publicity Margo Flynn.

title_en: Luv Your Neighbor Eyes Breakthrough in Delaware Oaks
description: Luv Your Neighbor looks to get her first stakes victory in the June 13 Delaware Oaks (G3) at Delaware Park.

title_en: 5th Circuit Again Strikes Down HISA Enforcement Power
description: The 5th United States Circuit Court of Appeals again rules that the enforcement powers of the Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority are an unconstitutional delegation of power reserved for the government.

title_en: Harper's Corner Is First Winner for Speaker's Corner
description: The filly wins her racing debut June 12 in a 5-furlong maiden special weight at Laurel Park. Harper's Corner is owned by Charles Biggs and trained by Cathal Lynch.

title_en: Techwerkian Sails to Become Olympiad's First Winner
description: Techwerkian wins a 4 1/2-furlong, main-track maiden special weight gate to wire by 1 1/4 lengths June 12 at Belterra Park.

title_en: Sharp-Eyed Horseman John Greathouse Jr. Dies at 82
description: John Greathouse Jr. helped his family-owned and -operated Glencrest Farm see success at the highest level.

title_en: White Abarrio to Enter Stud in 2027 at Gainesway
description: White Abarrio, winner of the 2023 Breeders' Cup Classic (G1) and earner of more than $8.4 million, will enter stud at Gainesway in 2027, according to a social media post June 11 by co-owner C2 Racing Stable.

title_en: Harrell Family Announces Opening of Reverie Farm
description: Curtis and Beth Harrell have reopened a historic 370-acre farm, formerly the site of Bonne Chance Farm, as a boutique boarding operation for broodmares under the management of John Durr.

title_en: Stallion Mind Your Biscuits Dies in Japan at 13
description: The son of Posse died suddenly June 9 at Shadai Stallion Station in Hokkaido, Japan, according to several international news outlets.

title_en: Sahadi, Owner of Cardiff Stud Farm, Dies at 91
description: A major owner and breeder in California, Fred Sahadi built Cardiff Stud Farm, co-founded Barretts Equine Sales, and was the father of trainer Jenine Sahadi.

title_en: Englishman Surges to Head of Maxfield's First Crop
description: In this week's Porter on Pedigrees, Englishman emerged as the star of Maxfield's first crop with an impressive performance in the June 6 Woody Stephens Stakes (G1) at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Coolmore Partners Officially Announce Purchase of Nysos
description: Coolmore partners Susan Magnier, Michael Tabor, and Derrick Smith, announced June 8 they have purchased multiple grade 1 winner Nysos and will race him in partnership with the Baoma Corp. through the 2026 racing season.

title_en: Structor Sires First Winner in Japan
description: American grade 1 winner and freshman stallion Structor, who stands at Lex Stud on Hokkaido in Japan, sired his first winner June 3, when his daughter Yakumo Venus won her racing debut at Sonoda Racecourse.

title_en: Coolmore and Newgate Combine to Stand Wodeton in 2026
description: Powerhouse operations Coolmore and Newgate Farm have teamed up to stand classy operator Wodeton for the 2026 breeding season.

title_en: Stone Farm's 50 Years of Breeding Excellence Continues
description: Stone Farm's legendary half-century history of producing top racehorses—steeped in the Hancock family's renowned Kentucky breeding heritage—continues with Growth Equity.

title_en: Tapit Has Opportunity to Build on Belmont Legacy
description: Gainesway's marquee sire Tapit is tied with Lexington as the leading sire of Belmont Stakes winners with four. Lexington's Belmont winners won the race between 1868 and 1878.

title_en: Stakes Winner Tap Into This to Stand at Valor Farm
description: Tap Into This, a son of leading sire Tapit, never finished out of the money in 10 career starts and had his racing career ended abruptly by a suspensory injury. He hails from the immediate family of Eclipse champion grass mare Islington.

title_en: Accomplished Owner/Breeder Susan Casner Dies May 27
description: Casner was instrumental in helping her husband and trainer Bill Casner develop a successful racing stable and was later involved in the development of WinStar Farm, a venture started by Bill Casner and Kenny Troutt.

title_en: Om N Joy Traces Back to Full Sister of Mr. Prospector
